message:
fix(franken_decision, franken_evidence): reject non-finite values and validate on deserialization

franken_decision:
- Add ValidationError::InvalidLoss and InvalidPosteriorProbability variants
  to catch NaN/Inf inputs that would silently corrupt expected-loss
  calculations and Bayesian updates
- LossMatrix::new() now rejects non-finite values before the existing
  negativity check, so NaN no longer sneaks past the < 0.0 guard
- Posterior::new() now rejects negative and non-finite probabilities
  before the normalization check
- FallbackPolicy::new() adds is_finite() guards to all three threshold
  fields so NaN/Inf thresholds cannot disable fallback detection
- Implement custom Deserialize for LossMatrix, Posterior, and
  FallbackPolicy that route through the validating constructors, closing
  the deserialization bypass where serde could construct invalid instances
  from untrusted JSON/TOML without hitting any validation
- Add tests: NaN loss matrix, NaN/negative posterior probabilities,
  NaN e-process threshold, and a JSON round-trip that confirms invalid
  values are rejected at deserialization time

franken_evidence:
- Parallel hardening (non-finite rejection and validating Deserialize
  impls) for the evidence ledger types to maintain consistency with the
  decision crate's invariants

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
